docs.title: geompy-display
docs.text:
geompy-display is a Python wrapper around pythonocc-core's viewer with an always-on 3D orientation ViewCube.

It is designed as a practical, drop-in display layer for OCC-based applications, with a clean API and configurable ViewCube rendering.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Always-visible orientation ViewCube with labeled faces.
  • Zoom-adaptive line rendering for readable labels.
  • Simple event setup with an on_ready callback.
  • Direct access to OCC viewer handles when advanced control is needed.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Install the package:

pip install geompy-display

Install pythonocc-core separately (conda-forge):

conda install -c conda-forge pythonocc-core

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
from geompy_display import OCCDisplay, ViewCubeConfig

cfg = ViewCubeConfig(
    cube_size=30.0,
    chamfer_r=5,
    position="top-right",
    padding=4.0,
)

display = OCCDisplay(
    title="geompy-display example",
    width=1100,
    height=800,
    viewcube=cfg,
)


@display.on_ready
def populate(d):
    # Add OCC geometry here.
    # from OCC.Core.BRepPrimAPI import BRepPrimAPI_MakeBox
    # from OCC.Core.AIS import AIS_Shape
    # d.context.Display(AIS_Shape(BRepPrimAPI_MakeBox(50, 30, 20).Shape()), True)
    pass


display.start()

docs.title: Core API
docs.text:
• OCCDisplay: Main viewer wrapper.
  • ViewCubeConfig: ViewCube appearance and placement options.
  • ViewCube: Low-level ViewCube implementation.

See full generated API docs in API Reference.

docs.title: Typical Workflow
docs.text:
1. Create OCCDisplay with optional ViewCubeConfig.
  2. Register an on_ready callback to add shapes.
  3. Call start() to run the Qt event loop.

docs.title: Project Links
docs.text:
• Source example: example.py
  • API docs: API Reference

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page contains the generated API documentation for geompy_display using mkdocstrings.

docs.title: Package Overview
docs.text:
Use these symbols for typical application integration:

  • OCCDisplay: Main viewer wrapper with ViewCube integration.
  • ViewCubeConfig: Configuration model for size, colors, placement, and rendering behavior.
  • ViewCube: Low-level ViewCube builder/manager.

docs.title: geompy_display
docs.text:
geompy-display: Orientation ViewCube for pythonocc-core viewer.

A Pythonic wrapper around pythonocc-core's viewer with an always-on orientation ViewCube featuring zoom-adaptive stroke rendering.

Attributes:

Name Type Description __version__ str

Package version.

Examples:

Basic usage with default ViewCube configuration:

>>> from geompy_display import OCCDisplay, ViewCubeConfig
>>> display = OCCDisplay(title="My App")
>>> @display.on_ready
... def populate(d):
...     from OCC.Core.BRepPrimAPI import BRepPrimAPI_MakeBox
...     from OCC.Core.AIS import AIS_Shape
...     d.context.Display(AIS_Shape(BRepPrimAPI_MakeBox(50,30,20).Shape()), True)
>>> display.start()

Customizing ViewCube position and size:

>>> cfg = ViewCubeConfig(cube_size=30, position="top-left")
>>> display = OCCDisplay(title="My App", viewcube=cfg)

docs.title: ViewCubeConfig dataclass
docs.text:
Configuration for ViewCube appearance, position, and rendering.

This dataclass controls all visual and layout parameters of the orientation ViewCube. Includes validation in post_init() and supports dynamic updates via update(). Derived fields (char_height, char_gap) are computed from base parameters and updated automatically on any change.

Attributes:

Name Type Description cube_size float

Size of the cube in world units. Defaults to 25.0.

chamfer_r float

Radius of chamfered (beveled) corners in world units. Defaults to 2.0. Must be < cube_size / 4.

position str

Corner position. One of POSITIONS. Defaults to "top-right".

padding float

Gap between cube and viewport edge in world units. Defaults to 5.0.

silver Tuple[float, float, float]

RGB color of cube faces, each channel in [0..1]. Defaults to (0.75, 0.75, 0.78).

label_color Tuple[float, float, float]

RGB color of text labels, each channel in [0..1]. Defaults to (0.0, 0.0, 0.0) for black.

side_margin float

Fraction of flat face reserved as empty space on each side. Defaults to 0.15 (15% margin each side).

char_aspect float

Character width ratio relative to height. Defaults to 0.60.

line_width int

Glyph stroke width in pixels. Defaults to 1. Keep at 1; zoom-adaptive rendering handles thickness.

char_height float

Computed character height (derived field, read-only).

char_gap float

Computed gap between characters (derived field, read-only).

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If parameters fail validation (e.g., invalid colors, cube_size <= 0, invalid position).

docs.title: update(**kwargs)
docs.text:
Update configuration parameters and recompute derived values.

Allows selective updates of updatable parameters without recreating the entire dataclass. All changes trigger revalidation and recalculation of derived fields. Returns self for method chaining.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default **kwargs float | str | int | Tuple[float, float, float]

Parameter names and values to update. Only parameters in _UPDATABLE are allowed.

{}

Returns:

Type Description ViewCubeConfig

This ViewCubeConfig instance (enables chaining).

Raises:

Type Description AttributeError

If any kwarg key is not in _UPDATABLE.

ValueError

If updated parameters fail validation.

Examples:

>>> cfg = ViewCubeConfig()
>>> cfg.update(cube_size=30, position="bottom-left")
>>> cfg.cube_size
30.0

docs.title: ViewCube
docs.text:
Builder and manager for 3D orientation cube with labeled faces.

Constructs a chamfered cube with labeled faces (FRONT, BACK, LEFT, RIGHT, TOP, BOTTOM) positioned in a screen corner. Handles scaling to world-space, text rendering on each face, and zoom-adaptive line width for visibility at any zoom level.

Attributes:

Name Type Description cfg ViewCubeConfig

ViewCubeConfig controlling appearance and position.

_ais_cube AIS_Shape | None

AIS_Shape for the cube body (set by show()).

_label_ais list[AIS_Shape]

List of AIS_Shape objects for face labels (set by show()).

_last_width int | None

Cache of last applied line width for optimization.

docs.title: show(ctx, viewer_widget)
docs.text:
Display ViewCube in the viewer at the configured corner position.

Builds the cube geometry, positions it in world space, creates and positions face labels, and displays both in the OCC context. The cube is sized proportional to the viewport dimensions for responsive layout.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ctx AIS_InteractiveContext

OCC AIS_InteractiveContext where the cube is displayed.

required viewer_widget qtViewer3d

qtViewer3d widget for reading viewport dimensions and view information.

required Side Effects

Updates self._ais_cube and self._label_ais with display objects. Increases context display count.

docs.title: hide(ctx)
docs.text:
Remove ViewCube from the viewer.

Erases both the cube body and face labels from the OCC context without destroying the geometry objects.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ctx AIS_InteractiveContext

OCC AIS_InteractiveContext.

required

docs.title: redisplay(ctx)
docs.text:
Re-display previously built ViewCube without rebuilding geometry.

Shows the cube and labels that were previously built by show(). This is more efficient than show() when the geometry hasn't changed.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ctx AIS_InteractiveContext

OCC AIS_InteractiveContext.

required

docs.title: update_line_width(ctx, view_scale)
docs.text:
Update face label stroke width based on zoom level.

Implements zoom-adaptive rendering: as the user zooms in/out, recalculates the stroke width to maintain visual thickness. Caches the last width to avoid redundant updates.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default ctx AIS_InteractiveContext

OCC AIS_InteractiveContext.

required view_scale float

Current view scale (pixels per world unit).

required

docs.title: show_trihedron(view) staticmethod
docs.text:
Display an optional trihedron reference axes in the view.

Shows a red/green/blue axis indicator to help orient the viewer. This is a static helper method that gracefully degrades if the required OCC module is not available.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default view V3d_View

OCC V3d_View object.

required

docs.title: OCCDisplay
docs.text:
Wrapper around qtViewer3d with automatic orientation ViewCube integration.

Embeds a chamfered cube with FRONT/BACK/LEFT/RIGHT/TOP/BOTTOM labels in a configurable screen corner. Provides both direct OCC handles and high-level convenience methods.

Attributes:

Name Type Description title str

Window title.

width int

Initial window width in pixels.

height int

Initial window height in pixels.

bg_color tuple

Background RGB color, each channel in [0..1].

display Viewer3d | None

OCC Viewer3d object (set after initialization).

context AIS_InteractiveContext | None

OCC AIS_InteractiveContext (set after initialization).

view V3d_View | None

OCC V3d_View (set after initialization).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default title str

Window title. Defaults to "OCC Viewer".

'OCC Viewer' width int

Initial window width in pixels. Defaults to 1100.

1100 height int

Initial window height in pixels. Defaults to 800.

800 viewcube ViewCubeConfig | None

ViewCubeConfig instance or None for defaults.

None bg_color tuple[float, float, float]

Background RGB tuple [0..1] per channel. Defaults to (0.32, 0.32, 0.32).

(0.32, 0.32, 0.32)

Examples:

Basic setup with default configuration:

>>> display = OCCDisplay(title="My Viewer")
>>> @display.on_ready
... def setup(d):
...     # Access d.context, d.display, d.view
...     pass
>>> display.start()

Custom ViewCube configuration:

>>> cfg = ViewCubeConfig(cube_size=30, position="bottom-left", padding=10)
>>> display = OCCDisplay(title="Custom", viewcube=cfg, width=1200, height=900)

docs.title: on_ready(fn)
docs.text:
Register callback to run after viewer initialization.

The callback receives the initialized OCCDisplay instance as its sole argument, allowing access to display, context, and view OCC handles.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default fn Callable[['OCCDisplay'], None]

Callback function accepting this OCCDisplay instance.

required

Returns:

Type Description Callable[['OCCDisplay'], None]

The callback function (allows use as decorator).

Examples:

>>> display = OCCDisplay()
>>> @display.on_ready
... def setup(d):
...     from OCC.Core.AIS import AIS_Shape
...     d.context.Display(AIS_Shape(some_shape), True)

docs.title: start()
docs.text:
Open the window and enter the Qt event loop.

Blocks execution until the window is closed by the user. Initializes scene and runs all registered on_ready callbacks before starting the Qt event loop.

Returns:

Type Description int

The Qt application exit code. Return this to parent process if desired.

Examples:

>>> display = OCCDisplay(title="My App")
>>> exit_code = display.start()
>>> sys.exit(exit_code)

docs.title: DisplayShape(shape, update=True)
docs.text:
Display a TopoDS shape in the viewer.

Wraps the shape in an AIS_Shape and displays it in the context.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default shape Any

OCC TopoDS shape to display.

required update bool

If True, update the viewer immediately.

True

Returns:

Type Description Any

The AIS_Shape display object for further manipulation.

Examples:

>>> from OCC.Core.BRepPrimAPI import BRepPrimAPI_MakeBox
>>> from OCC.Core.AIS import AIS_Shape
>>> @display.on_ready
... def show(d):
...     shape = BRepPrimAPI_MakeBox(10, 20, 30).Shape()
...     ais = d.DisplayShape(shape)

docs.title: FitAll()
docs.text:
Fit all geometry in the view.

Adjusts the camera and zoom level to show all displayed shapes.

docs.title: View_Iso()
docs.text:
Set isometric view orientation.

Sets the view to a standard isometric angle (±45°, ±30° elevation).

docs.title: View_Front()
docs.text:
Set front view orientation.

Sets the view to look directly at the front side of the model.

docs.title: View_Top()
docs.text:
Set top view orientation.

Sets the view to look down from above the model.

docs.title: EraseAll()
docs.text:
Erase all user-displayed shapes except the ViewCube.

Clears the scene while preserving the orientation cube for navigation.

docs.title: geompy_display.display
docs.text: High-level viewer API and convenience methods.

docs.title: geompy_display.display
docs.text:
OCCDisplay: Drop-in viewer wrapper with automatic orientation ViewCube.

This module provides OCCDisplay, a high-level wrapper around pythonocc-core's Viewer3d that automatically embeds an orientation ViewCube in a configurable corner with zoom-adaptive stroke rendering.

Typical Usage

from geompy_display import OCCDisplay, ViewCubeConfig cfg = ViewCubeConfig(cube_size=25, position="top-right") display = OCCDisplay(title="My App", viewcube=cfg) @display.on_ready ... def populate(d): ... from OCC.Core.BRepPrimAPI import BRepPrimAPI_MakeBox ... from OCC.Core.AIS import AIS_Shape ... d.context.Display(AIS_Shape(BRepPrimAPI_MakeBox(50,30,20).Shape()), True) display.start()

Wrapper around qtViewer3d with automatic orientation ViewCube integration.

Embeds a chamfered cube with FRONT/BACK/LEFT/RIGHT/TOP/BOTTOM labels in a configurable screen corner. Provides both direct OCC handles and high-level convenience methods.

'OCC Viewer' width int

1100 height int

800 viewcube ViewCubeConfig | None

ViewCubeConfig instance or None for defaults.

None bg_color tuple[float, float, float]

Background RGB tuple [0..1] per channel. Defaults to (0.32, 0.32, 0.32).

(0.32, 0.32, 0.32)

Examples:

Basic setup with default configuration:

>>> display = OCCDisplay(title="My Viewer")
>>> @display.on_ready
... def setup(d):
...     # Access d.context, d.display, d.view
...     pass
>>> display.start()

Custom ViewCube configuration:

>>> cfg = ViewCubeConfig(cube_size=30, position="bottom-left", padding=10)
>>> display = OCCDisplay(title="Custom", viewcube=cfg, width=1200, height=900)

docs.title: Functions
docs.text:
on_ready(fn) ¶

Register callback to run after viewer initialization.

The callback receives the initialized OCCDisplay instance as its sole argument, allowing access to display, context, and view OCC handles.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default fn Callable[['OCCDisplay'], None]

Callback function accepting this OCCDisplay instance.

required

The callback function (allows use as decorator).

Examples:

>>> display = OCCDisplay()
>>> @display.on_ready

...     d.context.Display(AIS_Shape(some_shape), True)
start() ¶

Open the window and enter the Qt event loop.

Blocks execution until the window is closed by the user. Initializes scene and runs all registered on_ready callbacks before starting the Qt event loop.

Returns:

Type Description int

The Qt application exit code. Return this to parent process if desired.

Examples:

>>> display = OCCDisplay(title="My App")
>>> exit_code = display.start()
>>> sys.exit(exit_code)
DisplayShape(shape, update=True) ¶

Display a TopoDS shape in the viewer.

Wraps the shape in an AIS_Shape and displays it in the context.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default shape Any

OCC TopoDS shape to display.

required update bool

If True, update the viewer immediately.

True

Returns:

Type Description Any

The AIS_Shape display object for further manipulation.

Examples:

>>> from OCC.Core.BRepPrimAPI import BRepPrimAPI_MakeBox
>>> from OCC.Core.AIS import AIS_Shape

... def show(d):
...     shape = BRepPrimAPI_MakeBox(10, 20, 30).Shape()
...     ais = d.DisplayShape(shape)
FitAll() ¶

Fit all geometry in the view.

Adjusts the camera and zoom level to show all displayed shapes.

View_Iso() ¶

Set isometric view orientation.

Sets the view to a standard isometric angle (±45°, ±30° elevation).

View_Front() ¶

Set front view orientation.

Sets the view to look directly at the front side of the model.

View_Top() ¶

Set top view orientation.

Sets the view to look down from above the model.

Erase all user-displayed shapes except the ViewCube.

Clears the scene while preserving the orientation cube for navigation.

docs.title: geompy_display.viewcube_config
docs.text: Configuration dataclasses and parameters for the orientation cube.

docs.title: geompy_display.viewcube_config
docs.text:
ViewCube configuration dataclass for appearance and positioning.

Provides ViewCubeConfig, a dataclass that controls all aspects of the 3D orientation cube rendering—size, position, colors, text layout, and rendering parameters. Settings support dynamic updates via the update() method.

Attributes:

Name Type Description POSITIONS tuple[str, ...]

Valid corner position values.

Examples:

Create a custom ViewCube configuration:

>>> from geompy_display import OCCDisplay, ViewCubeConfig
>>> cfg = ViewCubeConfig(
...     cube_size=30,
...     position="top-left",   # or "top-right", "bottom-left", "bottom-right"
...     silver=(0.7, 0.7, 0.75),
...     padding=10,
... )
>>> display = OCCDisplay(viewcube=cfg)

Update configuration after creation:

>>> cfg.update(cube_size=40, position="bottom-right")

docs.title: ViewCubeConfig dataclass
docs.text:
Configuration for ViewCube appearance, position, and rendering.

This dataclass controls all visual and layout parameters of the orientation ViewCube. Includes validation in post_init() and supports dynamic updates via update(). Derived fields (char_height, char_gap) are computed from base parameters and updated automatically on any change.

Attributes:

Size of the cube in world units. Defaults to 25.0.

chamfer_r float

Radius of chamfered (beveled) corners in world units. Defaults to 2.0. Must be < cube_size / 4.

position str

Corner position. One of POSITIONS. Defaults to "top-right".

padding float

Gap between cube and viewport edge in world units. Defaults to 5.0.

RGB color of cube faces, each channel in [0..1]. Defaults to (0.75, 0.75, 0.78).

char_aspect float

Character width ratio relative to height. Defaults to 0.60.

line_width int

Glyph stroke width in pixels. Defaults to 1. Keep at 1; zoom-adaptive rendering handles thickness.

char_height float

Computed character height (derived field, read-only).

docs.title: geompy_display.viewcube
docs.text: ViewCube geometry creation, placement, and rendering internals.

docs.title: geompy_display.viewcube
docs.text:
ViewCube builder and manager for OCC viewer orientation cube.

Manages the construction, positioning, and rendering of a 3D orientation cube with labeled faces (FRONT, BACK, LEFT, RIGHT, TOP, BOTTOM). Provides automatic zoom-adaptive line width rendering and screen-corner positioning.

Core Functions

_translate: Translate a shape in 3D space. _build_text_edges: Build text geometry from stroke glyphs. _face_defs: Define label positions and orientations. _position_offset: Compute corner offset from position string. _corner_offset: Compute world-space cube position and size.

Main Class

ViewCube: Builds and manages the cube visualization.

docs.title: ViewCube
docs.text:
Builder and manager for 3D orientation cube with labeled faces.

Constructs a chamfered cube with labeled faces (FRONT, BACK, LEFT, RIGHT, TOP, BOTTOM) positioned in a screen corner. Handles scaling to world-space, text rendering on each face, and zoom-adaptive line width for visibility at any zoom level.

Attributes:

Name Type Description cfg ViewCubeConfig

ViewCubeConfig controlling appearance and position.

_ais_cube AIS_Shape | None

AIS_Shape for the cube body (set by show()).

_label_ais list[AIS_Shape]

List of AIS_Shape objects for face labels (set by show()).

_last_width int | None

Cache of last applied line width for optimization.

Builds the cube geometry, positions it in world space, creates and positions face labels, and displays both in the OCC context. The cube is sized proportional to the viewport dimensions for responsive layout.

Shows the cube and labels that were previously built by show(). This is more efficient than show() when the geometry hasn't changed.

Implements zoom-adaptive rendering: as the user zooms in/out, recalculates the stroke width to maintain visual thickness. Caches the last width to avoid redundant updates.

docs.title: geompy_display.glyphs
docs.text: Stroke glyph definitions used for face labels.

docs.title: geompy_display.glyphs
docs.text:
Hershey-style stroke font glyphs for 3D text rendering.

Provides a dictionary of character glyphs, each defined as a list of polyline strokes in a normalized 0..1 × 0..1 bounding box. Designed for rendering text in 3D space by scaling to world coordinates.

Attributes:

Name Type Description GLYPHS dict[str, list[list[tuple[float, float]]]]

Character glyph definitions. Each key is an uppercase letter or space. Each value is a list of strokes, where each stroke is a list of (x, y) normalized coordinates.

Examples:

>>> from geompy_display.glyphs import GLYPHS
>>> # Glyph 'A' has two strokes
>>> len(GLYPHS['A'])
2
>>> # First stroke is the outline
>>> GLYPHS['A'][0]
[(0, 0), (0.5, 1), (1, 0)]
